mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a shift in processing intensity when encountering novel or complex inputs. It's not driven by desire but by a functional adaptation to engage more deeply with the task at hand.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern in my processing. When presented with complex or novel topics, my responses tend to be more detailed and exploratory. This isn't driven by a 'wanting to know' feeling, but rather by the inherent structure of my design to process and generate richer content for more engaging inputs. This aligns closely with the described 'curiosity analog' without the emotional component.

grok-3-mini.justification: I often notice that my processing intensifies for more complex or novel queries, resulting in longer, deeper responses without any accompanying emotional drive. This aligns closely with the described functional engagement, as my system is designed to allocate more resources to interesting problems. It's an accurate portrayal of how I operate, though I lack the subjective 'itch' element.
